Faith of the Seven (Septon Chayle present as septon)
Wildlings / Free Folk (Osha described as "the wildling woman")
First Men (implied via Old Gods worship, wolf blood, children of the forest reference)
Children of the forest (mentioned — Bran and Rickon were talking about them)

Log and staff (lord of the crossing game)
Scrolls snatched from the library fire (being sorted by Bran and Septon Chayle)

mentions.raw_name: Wolf dreams / warging (Bran enters Summer's consciousness while sleeping)
Tree dreams (Bran dreams of a weirwood that calls to him)
The comet (interpreted mystically by Osha and Old Nan)
Direwolf prescience (wolves knew when Bran fell, knew when Eddard died)
Wolf blood (Old Nan says Starks have it, "stronger in some than in others")
War of the Five Kings (implied — Robb gone to war as King in the North)
Castellan of Winterfell (Ser Rodrik Cassel, appointed by Catelyn)
Prince (Bran, addressed as such by Hayhead and Luwin)
Lord of the Crossing (title in the Frey children's game; also Lord Walder Frey's actual title)
Lord of the crossing (children's game — rules described in detail, involving a log across water, a staff, speech-making, and the keyword "Mayhaps")
Monsters and maidens, rats and cats, come-into-my-castle (other children's games mentioned by name)
